methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $103M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Zeitview
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series D)

data.narrative:
Oxa is a Series D autonomous systems company founded by Paul Newman and Ingmar Posner, operating in the Drones & Autonomous sector alongside five other private peers including Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air. The company has raised $103M to date. The comp universe in this sector remains immature, with no public comparables and insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage private peers meeting minimum data density thresholds for reliable peer-median analysis.

Given data constraints, the Stage-Progression method is the applicable triangulation approach. Using total capital raised ($103M) as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D progression yields an implied valuation of $154M. This method assumes Oxa's growth trajectory and capital efficiency align with sector norms at this stage.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $131M to $178M, centered on $154M. The range reflects ±15% variance around the stage-progression midpoint to account for execution risk and company-specific performance relative to peer cohort. Primary uncertainties stem from the absence of transaction-level data on same-stage or adjacent-stage peers, precluding validation via market comparables. Resolution of valuation depends on clarifying Oxa's revenue trajectory, gross margins, and capital efficiency relative to the broader autonomous systems peer set as market data matures.
